scope.purpose: PlayBridge TV control-path wizard filtering and backend recommendation.

control_backend_policy.sony_bravia: Preferred for Sony BRAVIA IP-control-capable TVs; ADB is fallback on Google TV models.
control_backend_policy.samsung_command: Preferred for Samsung Tizen local paired command where implemented.
control_backend_policy.smartthings: Samsung cloud/account fallback; experimental in PlayBridge.
control_backend_policy.adb: Preferred for Android TV / Google TV after developer options and device authorization.
control_backend_policy.roku_ecp: Preferred for Roku TV models via ECP on local network port 8060.
control_backend_policy.custom_command: Fallback for VIDAA, Fire TV variants, unsupported models, and user-defined integrations.

lineups.label: Sony BRAVIA IP control / Google TV
lineups.notes: Prefer Sony BRAVIA IP control for HDMI/input commands; ADB fallback on Google TV models.
lineups.region_notes: Lineup-level fallback region mapping. Prefer model-level regions when a matching model row exists.

lineups.label: Samsung Tizen paired command
lineups.notes: Use paired local Samsung command path first where available; SmartThings cloud is secondary/experimental.
lineups.region_notes: Lineup-level fallback region mapping. Prefer model-level regions when a matching model row exists.

lineups.region_notes: Lineup-level fallback region mapping. Prefer model-level regions when a matching model row exists.

lineups.region_notes: Lineup-level fallback for older TCL Android TV regional model families.

lineups.label: TCL China (雷鸟系统 / Android)
lineups.notes: China-domestic TCL and the FFALCON (雷鸟) sub-brand run Android-based 雷鸟系统; ADB over the network is a candidate where developer mode / network ADB is permitted, otherwise a custom command. Confirm per model.
lineups.region_notes: China-domestic market lineup; control path is a low-confidence candidate pending hardware confirmation.

lineups.label: Hisense China (VIDAA / 聚好看)
lineups.notes: China-domestic Hisense runs VIDAA / 聚好看 (Hi-Table); there is no first-class PlayBridge VIDAA backend, so use a custom command, with ADB as a candidate fallback on Android-capable sets. Confirm per model.
lineups.region_notes: China-domestic market lineup; control path is a low-confidence candidate pending hardware confirmation.

lineups.label: Hisense China (Android / JUUI)
lineups.notes: China-domestic Hisense models on an Android-based China UI (JUUI), distinct from international VIDAA / Google TV. No first-class PlayBridge backend; HDMI-CEC is the recommended on-device control but is not a PlayBridge backend, so a custom command is primary with ADB as a candidate/experimental fallback. Candidate mapping, not hardware-validated.
lineups.region_notes: China-domestic market lineup; control path is a low-confidence candidate pending hardware confirmation.

models.name: E8N Pro (ULED X Mini LED)
models.notes: China-market Hisense E8N Pro (65/75/85/100-inch; ULED X Mini LED, 144Hz native; includes the Black Myth: Wukong co-branded variant). Android-based China UI (JUUI), NOT Google TV or international VIDAA. On-device, HDMI-CEC is the recommended control and ADB is a candidate/experimental path where network ADB is permitted; PlayBridge has no CEC backend, so a custom command is the primary path with ADB as fallback. Candidate mapping from an operator-supplied spec, not hardware-validated.
models.region_notes: China-domestic release; size SKUs 65E8N Pro / 75E8N Pro / 85E8N Pro / 100E8N Pro confirmed per model during wizard search.

fact_check_notes: Converted from size-specific sample rows to model-family rows; sizes are stored as 'various' unless the family is size-specific.
Removed 2018-2021 model rows from the new output because the requested scope is 2022-2025.
Kept all rows validated=false because no physical TV hardware validation was performed.
Added region/platform/backend fields so the wizard can choose a control path without inferring only from a display name.
Sony 2022-2025 models are mapped to sony_bravia first, with adb fallback, because BRAVIA IP control is more purpose-specific than generic ADB for HDMI/input switching.
Hisense rows are split by region because North American U-series models commonly run Google TV while UK/EU/global equivalents commonly run VIDAA.
TCL rows are split between US Roku/Google TV families and global/UK/EU/Asia Google TV families.
Older non-top-5 brands from the uploaded sample, such as Roku, Vizio, and Panasonic, are not in the revised core output because this request targeted the top TV-brand control database.

Schema v2 adds region metadata. Each model row includes regions/release_regions using allowed values US, UK, EU, Asia. Each lineup row also includes regions/release_regions as fallback for wizard filtering.
Region mapping remains family-level and not exact per-size/per-retailer SKU. Use regional suffix/model-code verification during hardware validation.
Expanded on 2026-05-31 from 2022-2025 to 2018-2025. Rows remain candidate model-family mappings, not hardware validation.
2018-2021 additions intentionally use model-family names and broad region buckets; exact regional suffixes/retailer SKUs must be confirmed during wizard search or user selection.

Added on 2026-06-02: China-domestic TCL (incl. the FFALCON / 雷鸟 sub-brand on 雷鸟系统 / Android) and Hisense (VIDAA / 聚好看) model families under a new CN region. Control-path mappings are low-confidence candidates - China-market OS variants gate ADB differently and have no first-class VIDAA backend - and remain validated=false pending hardware confirmation.

Added on 2026-06-03: Hisense E8N Pro (CN, 2024; ULED X Mini LED, incl. the Black Myth: Wukong co-branded variant) from an operator-supplied spec, under a new hisense-china-android lineup (Android-based China UI / JUUI, distinct from VIDAA/Google TV). HDMI-CEC is the on-device recommended control but is not a PlayBridge backend, so the row maps to custom_command primary + adb experimental fallback. validated=false, low confidence, pending hardware confirmation.

region_schema.meaning: Release markets/regions where the TV model family is known or reasonably mapped as sold/released. This is a family-level market field, not a guarantee that every exact size/SKU is available in every listed region.
region_schema.usage: Configurator should filter by region first, then brand/model family, then choose the model row primary_backend and fallback_backends. If the exact model is missing, use a lineup row whose regions include the selected region.
